  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Low-side pressure is abnormally low (or in a vacuum) while high-side pressure is abnormally high",
    "Indicates a severe restriction in the high-pressure liquid line or expansion device (TXV/orifice tube)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recover refrigerant according to EPA Section 609 guidelines",
    "Inspect and replace the restricted expansion valve (TXV) or orifice tube",
    "Replace the receiver-drier or accumulator-drier",
    "Evacuate and recharge the system to OEM specifications"
  ],
  "rationale": "A low-side pressure reading that is extremely low or in a vacuum, combined with an abnormally high high-side pressure, indicates a severe restriction in the refrigerant flow, typically at the expansion device (TXV or orifice tube). This prevents refrigerant from returning to the low side, starving the compressor and risking catastrophic compressor failure due to lack of oil return.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 609",
    "OEM A/C Pressure-Temperature Diagnostics Chart"
  ]
}
